outlook.headline: Hybrid bonding demand is broadening across advanced packaging
outlook.summary: Hybrid bonding looks set to strengthen over the next 1-2 quarters as more of the advanced-packaging toolchain cites it as a real pull factor, not just a roadmap item. Applied Materials, KLA, GlobalFoundries and Kulicke & Soffa all point to strengthening traction, while Onto and Entegris remain more neutral/strategic, suggesting the opportunity is real but still unevenly monetized. Watch whether the current product momentum converts from positioning and program acceleration into visible order growth.

drivers.label: Advanced packaging tool demand is rising
drivers.detail: Emerging hybrid-bonding and die-stack activity is increasing demand for advanced packaging tools, especially where tight supply supports pricing and utilization.

drivers.label: Packaging stack expansion at suppliers
drivers.detail: Hybrid bonding and NEXX are strengthening Applied Materials' packaging technology stack, indicating a broader product mix opportunity.

drivers.label: Strategic roadmap pull from foundry/customer side
drivers.detail: GlobalFoundries says hybrid bonding is part of its advanced packaging roadmap, signaling product pull and longer-run relevance for the ecosystem.

drivers.label: Program acceleration and competitive solution traction
drivers.detail: Kulicke & Soffa said it is confident in its solution and is accelerating the program, pointing to improving commercial momentum.

drivers.label: Near-term signal still partly strategic
drivers.detail: Onto Innovation had only a positioning conversation, and Entegris framed hybrid bonding as strategically important rather than a near-term demand driver.

risks: Adoption may stay roadmap-driven if positioning does not convert into orders quickly, especially for Onto Innovation and Entegris.
Any slowdown in emerging die-stack or advanced packaging activity would directly soften the demand signal seen at KLA and K&S.
If hybrid bonding remains concentrated in a few programs, the broadening trend could stall despite the positive mention trend.
